title: chrysocephalum apiculatum subsp. racemosum
intro: Chrysocephalum apiculatum subsp. racemosum, also known as Yellow Buttons, is a small, annual herb native to Australia, characterized by its bright yellow daisy-like flowers and slender stems. The plant is used in traditional medicine due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal properties, which make it effective in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Yellow Buttons include reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and boosting the immune system, while also being used to treat colds, fever, and respiratory problems. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, although its effectiveness and safety should be studied and confirmed by medical professionals.
